disclaimer: Information may change. Always verify current requirements with official Chinese embassy or consulate before travel. This guide is for general reference only and does not constitute specific legal advice.
visaFreeNote: Check current policy as rules may change; some short-term visits may be visa-free under specific agreements.

applicationSteps.title: Check current requirements
applicationSteps.detail: Visit the official website of the Chinese Embassy or Consulate in Canada for the latest visa policies and updates.

applicationSteps.title: Prepare required documents
applicationSteps.detail: Gather all necessary documents, ensuring your passport is valid for at least 6 months beyond your planned stay.

applicationSteps.title: Complete the application form
applicationSteps.detail: Fill out the China visa application form accurately, either online or on paper, and attach a recent passport photo.

applicationSteps.title: Submit application and pay fees
applicationSteps.detail: Submit your application in person or via an authorized service center, and pay the applicable visa fees.

applicationSteps.title: Track and collect visa
applicationSteps.detail: Monitor the application status and collect your visa once approved, or receive it by mail if available.

requiredDocuments: Valid Canadian passport with at least 6 months of remaining validity
Proof of accommodation in China (e.g., hotel booking or invitation letter)
Bank statement showing sufficient funds for the trip
processingTime: 4-7 business days typical, with express processing available for an additional fee.
entryPorts: Major international airports (e.g., Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou)
Some seaports for cruise or ferry arrivals

customsRules.limit: Declare if carrying more than $5,000 USD (or equivalent in other currencies) upon entry or exit.
Personal-use devices like laptops and phones are generally allowed, but high-value items may require declaration.
Up to 1.5 liters of alcohol per adult may be brought in duty-free; larger quantities are subject to duties.
Up to 400 cigarettes or 100 cigars per adult; excess quantities require declaration and payment of duties.

arrivalTips: Fill out the arrival card provided on the plane before landing.
Have your hotel address written in Chinese to show immigration officers.
Keep your passport easily accessible during immigration checks.
Learn basic Chinese phrases like 'hello' and 'thank you' for smoother interactions.
Have some local currency (Chinese Yuan, CNY) ready for immediate expenses upon arrival.
